question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a negative value via the HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR environment variable (e.g., '-10000') to bypass the only guard 'cfg.amountTinybar == 0' in config.go, causing the operator's transfer direction to reverse so the operator receives funds instead of sending them, effectively modifying the economic parameters of every scheduled transaction outside design intent?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR value set it to math.MaxInt64 (9223372036854775807), causing hiero.HbarFromTinybar() to receive an out-of-range tinybar value, resulting in a malformed transfer transaction that triggers unexpected fee computation or SDK panic, modifying effective transaction costs outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES environment variable set it to a very large integer (e.g., 2147483647) so that submitWithRetry() loops that many times per ticker tick, each attempt consuming a full transaction fee from the operator account, causing total fee expenditure to far exceed design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user who can set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to a minimal duration (e.g., '1ns' or '1ms') cause the ticker in main() to fire at maximum rate, submitting a transaction on every tick and multiplying total fee expenditure by orders of magnitude beyond the intended design interval of 1s, draining the operator account through fee accumulation?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user who sets HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to a value >= 64 cause the expression '1<<(attempt-1)' in backoff() to integer-overflow to zero or a negative duration, collapsing the backoff delay to near-zero and causing all retry attempts to fire in rapid succession, each consuming a transaction fee and multiplying total fee cost beyond design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF set it to '0s' or a negative duration, causing toDuration() to fall back to 1s but the validation 'cfg.baseBackoff <= 0' to reset it to 1s, and then verify whether a crafted value like '-1ns' bypasses the guard and causes backoff() to return zero, eliminating retry delays and causing all maxRetries attempts to fire instantly with full fee cost each time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK set it to 'other' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL pointing to an attacker-controlled mirror node server, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to fetch a crafted node list that routes all transactions to attacker-controlled gRPC endpoints, where the attacker can manipulate fee schedules or reject transactions to cause repeated retries and amplified fee spending?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST URL (HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST) serve a crafted JSON response with thousands of nodeEntry records each containing many serviceEndpoints, causing the network map to be populated with attacker-controlled addresses that the SDK will use for transaction submission, routing fee-bearing transactions to nodes that charge non-standard fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that NewTransferTransaction() in submitWithRetry() never calls SetMaxTransactionFee(), meaning the SDK uses its default maximum fee, and then craft a scenario where the attacker-controlled node returns a fee schedule with inflated fees that the SDK silently accepts up to its default cap, causing the operator to pay fees far above the intended design amount per transaction?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject command-line arguments (e.g., via a misconfigured container entrypoint) pass '-amount-tinybar=-1' to override the HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR default, bypassing the '== 0' check since -1 != 0, causing every transfer to move funds in the wrong direction and altering the effective economic parameters of all scheduled transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to a value that causes integer overflow when negated in 'hiero.HbarFromTinybar(-cfg.amountTinybar)' (e.g., math.MinInt64), producing an undefined or wrapped tinybar value that results in a transaction with an unexpected transfer amount and associated fee outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL set it to a value that toDuration() fails to parse, causing toDuration() to silently return 1s as a fallback, and then verify whether there is a scenario where the fallback duration is shorter than the intended design interval, causing more frequent transactions and higher cumulative fee expenditure?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST endpoint return a JSON payload with a 'nodes' array containing entries with duplicate or overlapping service_endpoints, causing the network map to be built with attacker-chosen gRPC addresses that override legitimate node addresses, routing transactions to fee-manipulating nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST URL cause buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to follow a redirect to an internal network address (SSRF), fetching a node list from an internal service that returns a crafted network map pointing to attacker-controlled gRPC endpoints that charge non-standard transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to 0 and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to '1ns', causing the pinger to submit one transaction per nanosecond tick with no retries, generating an unbounded volume of fee-bearing transactions that drain the operator account at a rate far outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the destination account (HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID) set it to the same account as the operator (HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID), causing a self-transfer that still incurs the full network transaction fee on every tick, effectively burning fees without any economic purpose and at a rate determined by the attacker-controlled interval?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to '0.5' or a non-integer string that strconv.ParseInt() rejects, causing loadConfig() to return an error and the pinger to fail to start, but if the default value '10000' is used as fallback instead, verify whether the flag.Func handler silently ignores parse errors and leaves amountTinybar at 0, bypassing the non-zero check?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to exactly 63, causing backoff() to compute '1<<62' which is a valid int64 but when multiplied by baseBackoff produces a duration that overflows time.Duration (int64 nanoseconds), resulting in a negative sleep duration that causes the timer to fire immediately, eliminating backoff delays and causing all 63 retries to execute at full speed with full fee cost?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror node REST endpoint return a response with Content-Type set to a non-JSON type but a JSON body, and verify whether json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode() still parses it, potentially accepting a crafted node list that routes transactions to fee-manipulating endpoints regardless of content type validation?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user who can write to /tmp/alive or /tmp/ready (shared /tmp filesystem in a container) replace these files with symlinks to sensitive paths, causing os.WriteFile() in main() to overwrite arbitrary files, potentially corrupting configuration that controls transaction fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to a very large duration (e.g., '8760h') causing the ticker to fire only once per year, effectively disabling the pinger's liveness function, but simultaneously set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to a very large value so that when the single annual transaction fires it transfers an amount far outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID set it to a non-existent or deleted account ID, causing every transaction to fail with INVALID_ACCOUNT_ID, triggering the full maxRetries retry loop on every tick, each failed attempt still consuming a transaction fee, multiplying fee expenditure by (maxRetries+1) per tick?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK set it to 'other' and provide a mirror REST URL that returns a node list with gRPC endpoints pointing to high-latency or unresponsive servers, causing every transaction attempt to time out and exhaust the full retry budget, with each timed-out attempt still consuming a transaction fee from the operator account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES to a very large value, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to retry the mirror node HTTP request an unbounded number of times during startup, and if the mirror node charges per-request fees or rate-limits in a way that causes the pinger to delay startup indefinitely, preventing the readiness probe from being written and causing the container to restart in a fee-consuming loop?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the mirror REST endpoint return a JSON response where node_account_id values are valid strings but map to system accounts (e.g., '0.0.2', '0.0.98'), causing the SDK to route transactions to system accounts that may apply different fee schedules than standard consensus nodes, modifying effective transaction fees outside design parameters?
